validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_architecture_parallel_section -v passes 10/10 (0.003s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 253/254 with 1 OS-specific skip (37.8s, up from 244 -- 10 new cases for unit_045)

checkpoint.summary: unit_045 complete. skills/development-harness/references/architecture.md gained a new top-level 'Parallel Execution Model' section with nine sub-sections that summarize the parallel pipeline end-to-end: When to enable parallelism (config knobs + 3-way candidate gate), Worktree-per-unit layout (.harness/worktrees/<batch_id>/<unit_id>/ + harness/<batch_id>/<unit_id> branches + WORKTREE_UNIT.json sentinel), Orchestrator / sub-agent boundary (Agent(subagent_type: 'harness-unit') + tight allowlist + 'self-report never trusted for blast radius'), Frontier + overlap check (select_next_unit.py --frontier + compute_parallel_batch.py + 5 packing constraints), Dispatch -> wait -> merge lifecycle (idle/dispatched/merging fleet.mode transitions + scope check BEFORE merge + stop-hook-never-sees-partial-fleet invariant), Conflict strategies (abort_batch vs serialize_conflicted + scope-violation cross-link), Merge serialization via .harness/.lock (O_EXCL + stale-lock takeover), Safety rails session kill switch (safety_rails.py + 2-failure threshold + session-scoped clearing), Observability (four .harness/logs/<batch_id>/ artifacts + best-effort semantics + /harness-state integration). Concludes with forward link to parallel-execution.md (unit_047). New test_architecture_parallel_section.py (160 LOC, 10 cases) pins every sub-heading + substantive tokens + position-order between Batch Semantics and Git Integration. Initial run caught an overly strict '"dispatched"' assertion that expected double-quote framing; relaxed to accept either `x`/'x' markdown-code or JSON-style quoting. Every claim in the new section is verified against a specific file in the repo -- the section is a true summary of what the code does, not aspirational docs. Suite 244 -> 254.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_046 (PHASE_011): update skills/development-harness/references/phase-contract.md to document required depends_on, touches_paths (when parallel_safe: true), and parallel_safe unit fields; add a 'Decomposing a phase for parallelism' subsection that walks through how to partition a phase into parallel-safe units. The existing phase-contract.md already has the 'Scope-Violation Enforcement Policy' section from unit_039 -- this unit extends the Units of Work contract with the parallelism-specific fields and adds the decomposition guidance. Validation: grep for new subsections + structural presence test similar to unit_045.
